commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=5e8dc95ba43d2bf42766a669d5ac89b6cc6e4482)
commands.review_summary:
The PR passes the current thinking setting into Anthropic message conversion, converts readable historical thinking blocks to text and drops redacted blocks when thinking is explicitly disabled, and adds a captured-payload regression test.

PR surface: Source +14, Tests +94. Total +108 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at the source level: a request can replay signed same-model thinking while omitting the current Anthropic thinking configuration, matching the linked persistent-session sequence. No live current-main Anthropic call was independently reproduced.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The author should cover the omitted-option path and supply redacted real persistent-session proof before maintainers reconsider merge readiness.

target.title: fix(anthropic): strip thinking blocks from history when thinking is disabled (fixes #92360)
target.body:
## What does this PR do?

Strip thinking blocks from conversation history when the current Anthropic API request has thinking explicitly disabled. This prevents persistent sessions from permanently breaking when the thinking configuration changes between runs.

## Related Issue

Fixes #92360

## Type of Change

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking)

## Changes Made

- `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts`: Added `thinkingEnabled` parameter to `convertMessages`; when `thinkingEnabled === false`, thinking blocks are converted to plain text (preserving context) and redacted thinking blocks are dropped
- `src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts`: Added test verifying thinking blocks are stripped when thinking is disabled

## How to Test

1. Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts` — all 31 tests pass
2. The new test "strips thinking blocks from history when thinking is explicitly disabled" verifies the fix

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** When a persistent named session accumulates thinking blocks from previous cron runs (thinking enabled) but the current request has thinking disabled, Anthropic rejects the request with a 400 because the history contains thinking content blocks without a matching thinking configuration. The session breaks permanently until manually reset.

**Real environment tested:** macOS 26.4.1, Node.js, OpenClaw repo at commit a740f742

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:**
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts
✓  unit  src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts (31 tests) 46ms
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  31 passed (31)

$ grep -n "thinkingEnabled === false" src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts
1233:          if (thinkingEnabled === false) {

$ grep -n "thinkingEnabled" src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts
1083:      options?.thinkingEnabled,
1170:  thinkingEnabled?: boolean,
1233:          if (thinkingEnabled === false) {
```

**Observed result after fix:** All 31 tests pass. The new test confirms that when `thinkingEnabled: false` is passed, thinking blocks with signatures are converted to plain text (type: "text") instead of being sent as `thinking` or `redacted_thinking` types. The fix is surgical — 12 lines of logic in `convertMessages` plus parameter plumbing.

**What was not tested:** Live Anthropic API call with a real persistent session. The fix is validated at the unit test level by capturing the actual API payload and asserting thinking blocks are stripped.
target.author: liuhao1024
